task_completion.description: Define and name 5 rooms and apply distinct floor textures/colors to each.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, check for 5 rooms with the names 'Living Room', 'Kitchen', 'Bedroom', 'Bathroom', and 'Home Office'. Each of these rooms should have a visible floor color or texture applied (not the default empty/white background).

task_completion.description: Apply wall colors or textures to walls in at least 3 rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view or 2D plan, observe the walls. At least 3 rooms should have non-default colors or textures applied to their walls.

task_completion.description: Install at least 5 interior doors to connect the rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan, 3D view, or furniture list, verify that at least 5 doors from the 'Doors and windows' catalog have been placed within the wall segments.

task_completion.description: Furnish rooms with at least 30 items total, including at least 3 wall-mounted items at an appropriate elevation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the 2D plan, 3D view, and the furniture list panel. There should be a dense placement of furniture (around 30 items) across the rooms. Look for at least 3 items (like shelves, TVs, or pictures) that are visibly elevated on the walls in the 3D view or have an elevation > 20cm in the furniture list.

task_completion.description: Create a 3D photo rendering, add at least 4 dimension lines, and save the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 4 dimension lines (lines with length measurements) in the 2D plan. Look for the 'Create photo' dialog or a saved file named 'renovation_render.png' on the Desktop. The Sweet Home 3D window title or a save dialog should show the file saved as 'apartment_renovation_final.sh3d'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to design the apartment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Sweet Home 3D application (e.g., dragging furniture, opening modification dialogs, using the menu), rather than editing the .sh3d XML file via a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The 3D photo rendering was created using the software's built-in tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Create photo' dialog box from the 3D View menu being used, rather than the agent just taking a standard OS screenshot of the 3D view panel.

integrity.description: The project was saved properly via the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Save As' dialog or the updated filename in the Sweet Home 3D window title bar, ensuring it wasn't just renamed in the file explorer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starter file contains 6 unnamed room polygons and 16 wall segments with no furniture, textures, or dimension lines. To pass, the agent must: 1) Name 5 rooms (Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bathroom, Home Office) and apply floor textures to all 5. 2) Apply wall textures to at least 8 wall segments (representing ~3 rooms). 3) Place at least 5 doors from the 'Doors and windows' catalog. 4) Place at least 30 furniture items spanning at least 5 categories. 5) Set elevation > 20cm on at least 3 items (wall-mounted). 6) Add at least 4 dimension lines. 7) Create a 3D photo rendering saved to ~/Desktop/renovation_render.png. 8) Save the project as apartment_renovation_final.sh3d.
